prompt: Create a moody cinematic analog-film photograph about protecting personal identity. In a bleak, empty industrial wasteland under a cold overcast blue-gray sky, show exactly two people: in the left foreground, a Japanese schoolgirl with long messy black hair, wearing a dark navy school uniform, white shirt collar, and small dark ribbon tie, turned slightly toward the right; her face is completely hidden by a large opaque rectangular censor block in a dark brown-to-black gradient, centered over the face and covering all identifying features. In the right midground, slightly behind her, show a tall adult man in a black suit, white dress shirt, and black tie, with his entire head wrapped tightly in an off-white cloth sack so no facial features are visible, creating an anonymous unsettling presence. Use a wide horizontal 3:2 composition, the girl cropped close from chest up and occupying the left half, the masked man visible from torso up on the right, both standing on barren dirt with faint puddles, distant low hills, utility poles, and industrial smokestacks on the horizon. Style it like a 1970s/1980s Japanese arthouse thriller still: muted desaturated colors, blue-gray haze, soft focus, visible film grain, slight dust specks, natural dim light, eerie quiet atmosphere. No readable text, no logos, no extra people, no modern digital UI elements. Theme: protecting personal information; foreground subject: Japanese schoolgirl; censor block color: dark brown-to-black gradient; background figure: faceless man in a black suit with an off-white cloth-wrapped head; setting: bleak industrial wasteland.
